name: Pyroclast
lore:
The Savvas live deep under the Copperneck Mountains, largely removed from the turmoil of the surface. There, they protect their source of life, which imbues unliving rock with consciousness and thought. The Savvas have few needs, and so, most are driven to seek domination over the elements, controlling and using wind, earth, ice, and fire. Those who are able to master the elements are held in the highest regard.

Savvas go to extreme lengths to exert their power over the elements. Some cast themselves into active volcanoes, where they are either destroyed or become one with the raw power of the magma, emerging as a Pyroclast—a living embodiment of the chaotic and destructive force of flowing lava. No one can question their power then.
summary: A high-complexity controller who shapes the battlefield with hazardous terrain and obstacles, punishing enemies with molten lava and inescapable terrain manipulation.
classNotes: You can create many hazardous terrain and obstacle tiles to damage and impede your enemies. When creating multiple overlay tiles, you are not required to create the maximum number indicated on the ability. Like any other class, the Pyroclast cannot create obstacles in hexes that would block off parts of the map.

There can never be more than **six** hexes of hazardous terrain you created on the map at any one time. If you would create a seventh, first remove any one existing hazardous terrain that you have created.

You have many abilities with mandatory persistent bonuses. Unlike other classes who can move cards with persistent bonuses to their discard pile (or lost pile if the action has a lost icon) whenever they want, you can **only** do so for cards with mandatory persistent bonuses at the start of your rests.
